message:
test(fuzz): add FcpcFrame seal/open AEAD round-trip + AAD-binding fuzz target (resolves 4cka5)

FcpcFrame::seal + open (fcpc.rs:169-294) wraps the control-plane payload
in ChaCha20-Poly1305 with AAD = session_id || seq || flags
(build_fcpc_aad fcpc.rs:311-317) and a directional nonce derived from
(seq, direction). Existing fuzz_fcpc_frame covers decode panic-freedom +
semantic invariants on accepted frames; the seal/open AEAD round-trip,
per-AAD-field binding, direction binding, key binding, and tamper
rejection are NOT covered.

A regression in seal/open could:
- drop direction from nonce → reflection attack (initiator-sent frame
  replayed back as if from responder)
- drop session_id from AAD → cross-session frame splice
- drop seq from AAD → AEAD-layer replay bypass distinct from ReplayWindow

Nine properties:
- seal→open round-trip
- direction binding (I→R cannot open R→I)
- key binding (alt k_ctx fails)
- AAD per-field binding ×3 (session_id, seq, flags) with no-op-mutation guard
- tamper rejection on ciphertext OR tag
- encode→decode→open round-trip preserves plaintext
- check_replay rejects same seq twice

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) Direction reflection: I→R-sealed frame opens cleanly under I→R
  but MUST fail under R→I (catches nonce direction byte drop at
  fcpc.rs:186)
- (b) Session-id splice: same seq + same key + different session_id
  MUST fail (catches session_id drop from AAD at fcpc.rs:313)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
